summary: AVDA is a framework that automates cybersecurity detection authoring by integrating organizational context into AI-assisted code generation, evaluating different agentic strategies for quality and cost.
rationale: The paper presents a framework for automating cybersecurity detection authoring, which is a systems-level problem involving AI-assisted code generation and integration into developer environments, directly contributing to the security of agentic systems.

title: AwesomeLit: Towards Hypothesis Generation with Agent-Supported Literature Research
summary: AwesomeLit is a human-agent collaborative visualization system that provides a transparent, user-steerable agentic workflow, a dynamic query exploring tree, and a semantic similarity view to help researchers generate hypotheses and explore unfamiliar topics.
rationale: The paper describes a human-agent collaborative system with transparent workflows and provenance visualization, directly addressing human oversight and trust in agentic literature research.

title: Backchaining Loss of Control Mitigations from Mission-Specific Benchmarks in National Security
summary: Proposes an empirical methodology for identifying and prioritizing affordance and permission-based mitigations for Loss of Control (LoC) threats in national security contexts by backchaining from errors on mission-specific benchmarks.
rationale: The paper directly addresses the engineering of permissions and affordances as safety levers for agentic systems in high-stakes environments, using an empirical methodology.

title: Baichuan-M4: A Clinical-Grade Medical Agent System for Continuous Care
summary: Presents Baichuan-M4, a clinical-grade medical agent system with a unified runtime for action constraints, tool use, long-term memory, and multi-agent coordination, designed for continuous care.
rationale: Describes an end-to-end agent architecture with specific systems-engineering components for safety, reliability, and continuous care in a high-stakes domain.

title: Beyond Context Sharing: A Unified Agent Communication Protocol (ACP) for Secure, Federated, and Autonomous Agent-to-Agent (A2A) Orchestration
summary: Proposes a standardized Agent Communication Protocol (ACP) for secure, federated, and autonomous agent-to-agent orchestration, including decentralized identity verification and semantic intent mapping.
rationale: Addresses secure and interoperable multi-agent coordination, a core systems challenge for trustworthy agentic systems.

title: Beyond Detection: Governing GenAI in Academic Peer Review as a Sociotechnical Challenge
summary: Examines the sociotechnical challenges of integrating GenAI into academic peer review, focusing on human oversight, accountability, and the division of labor between AI and human evaluative judgment, based on mixed-method empirical study.
rationale: Addresses human oversight and governance challenges in agentic workflows (peer review) through an empirical study, offering recommendations for preserving accountability and trust.

title: Beyond Fluency: Toward Reliable Trajectories in Agentic IR
summary: This position paper synthesizes failure modes in industrial agentic IR systems, categorizing errors across planning, retrieval, reasoning, and execution, and proposes verification gates and systematic abstention for trajectory integrity.
rationale: The paper provides a foundational analysis of failure modes in agentic IR systems, focusing on trajectory integrity and causal attribution, which is relevant to trustworthy agentic systems.

title: Beyond Retrieval: Modeling Confidence Decay and Deterministic Agentic Platforms in Generative Engine Optimization
summary: Proposes a deterministic multi-agent intent routing paradigm to mitigate probabilistic hallucinations in RAG-based Generative Engine Optimization, using a Deterministic Agent Handoff (DAH) protocol to route specific intents to specialized agents.
rationale: Addresses the reliability and safety of agentic systems by proposing a multi-agent coordination mechanism for deterministic intent routing, moving beyond probabilistic RAG.

title: Beyond Scaling: Assessing Strategic Reasoning and Rapid Decision-Making Capability of LLMs in Zero-sum Environments
summary: Introduces STAR Benchmark, a multi-agent evaluation framework for assessing LLM agents in zero-sum competitive environments, focusing on iterative decision-making, temporal constraints, and strategic behavior quality beyond win-loss outcomes.
rationale: The paper introduces a multi-agent evaluation framework for assessing strategic reasoning and rapid decision-making in interactive, adversarial environments, which is relevant to the observation and evaluation of agent behavior.

title: Blockchain and AI: Securing Intelligent Networks for the Future
summary: A taxonomy, integration patterns, and evaluation blueprint for securing intelligent networks using blockchain and AI, clarifying their respective contributions to provenance, trust, detection, and adaptation.
rationale: Provides a foundational synthesis, taxonomy, and evaluation framework for securing agentic workflows in intelligent networks, addressing cross-community framing and evaluation.

title: Brain-Inspired Graph Multi-Agent Systems for LLM Reasoning
summary: Proposes Brain-Inspired Graph Multi-Agent Systems (BIGMAS) where specialized LLM agents coordinate through a shared workspace with a global orchestrator and dynamic graph topology to improve complex multi-step reasoning.
rationale: This paper contributes to multi-agent coordination and orchestration, addressing a systems-level challenge in agentic reasoning beyond model-level improvements.

title: Building Better Environments for Autonomous Cyber Defence
summary: This paper provides a framework for decomposing the interface between RL cyber environments and real systems, along with guidelines for developing and evaluating RL-based autonomous cyber defense agents in network defense scenarios.
rationale: The paper contributes to the architectural understanding and evaluation of autonomous agents in a critical security domain, focusing on environment design for trustworthy agent behavior.

title: Bypassing Document Ingestion: An MCP Approach to Financial Q&A
summary: Explores Model Context Protocol (MCP) as a direct data interaction alternative to RAG for financial Q&A, exposing LSEG APIs as tools via a custom MCP server and evaluating its performance and limitations.
rationale: The paper directly addresses the design and evaluation of tool interfaces for agents, specifically using MCP to access curated data systems instead of document ingestion.

title: CAF-Gen: A Multi-Agent System for Enriching Argumentation Structures
summary: CAF-Gen is a multi-agent framework that uses an iterative Creator-Reviewer pipeline to enrich shallow argument structures into CAF-compliant models, improving structural integrity and data quality through multi-agent collaboration.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent coordination mechanism (Creator-Reviewer pipeline) to improve the reliability and structural integrity of agent-generated outputs, which is a systems-level contribution.

title: Can Blindfolded LLMs Still Trade? An Anonymization-First Framework for Portfolio Optimization
summary: A framework for evaluating LLM trading agents by anonymizing identifiers to validate signal legitimacy and assess robustness across market regimes, addressing memorization and survivorship biases.
rationale: The paper contributes to the rigorous evaluation of agent behavior by addressing spurious performance and validating signal legitimacy, which is a systems-level concern for trustworthy agents.

title: Can LLM Agents Simulate Dynamic Networks? A Case Study on Email Networks with Phishing Synthesis
summary: Introduces two extensions, data-driven event triggers and Hawkes processes, to LLM multi-agent simulation frameworks to improve the fidelity of macroscopic network dynamics for modeling information propagation and cybersecurity threats.
rationale: Addresses the reliability of multi-agent simulations for security applications by improving the realism of network dynamics, which is relevant for evaluating agentic systems.

title: Causal methods for LLM development and evaluation
summary: This paper argues for the application of causal inference methods to address inherent causal questions in LLM development and evaluation, including agentic workflows, to ensure reliable and scientifically grounded design.
rationale: The paper provides a foundational argument for applying causal methods to improve reliability in LLM development and evaluation, explicitly mentioning agentic workflows and evaluation pipelines.

title: Challenges and Future Directions in Agentic Reverse Engineering Systems
summary: An analysis of existing agentic reverse engineering systems, identifying limitations in token constraints, obfuscation handling, and program guardrails, and outlining future directions for system designers from a security perspective.
rationale: This paper provides a foundational analysis of challenges in agentic systems for a security-critical task, directly informing future trustworthy system design.

title: ChipMATE: Multi-Agent Training via Reinforcement Learning for Enhanced RTL Generation
summary: ChipMATE is a self-trained multi-agent framework for RTL generation that uses a Verilog agent and a Python reference-model agent for mutual verification, incorporating a backtrack-based inference workflow to prevent error propagation.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent coordination framework with a specific workflow for mutual verification and error prevention, addressing reliability in agentic systems.

title: ClawCoin: An Agentic AI-Native Cryptocurrency for Decentralized Agent Economies
summary: ClawCoin is a tokenized, compute-cost-indexed cryptocurrency designed to stabilize execution capacity and improve coordination in decentralized agent economies by providing a unit of account aligned with API token consumption.
rationale: This paper proposes a novel architectural component for decentralized agent economies, addressing the reliability of agent operations under compute cost shocks, which is a systems-level concern.

title: Clover: A Neural-Symbolic Agentic Harness with Stochastic Tree-of-Thoughts for Verified RTL Repair
summary: Clover is a neural-symbolic agentic harness that orchestrates RTL repair as a structured search, dynamically dispatching tasks to specialized LLM agents or symbolic solvers using a stochastic tree-of-thoughts for reliable outcomes.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent coordination system for RTL repair, focusing on reliability and structured search, which is a systems-level contribution to agentic trustworthiness.

title: Code Broker: A Multi-Agent System for Automated Code Quality Assessment
summary: Code Broker is a multi-agent system for automated code quality assessment, featuring a hierarchical architecture, asynchronous execution with retry logic, and session memory, presented as a technical report on system design and tool orchestration.
rationale: This paper describes a multi-agent system architecture for code quality assessment, including details on orchestration, retry logic, and memory, which are relevant to trustworthy agentic systems.

title: Cognitive Agency Surrender: Defending Epistemic Sovereignty via Scaffolded AI Friction
summary: Theorizes "Scaffolded Cognitive Friction" using multi-agent systems as cognitive forcing functions to counteract automation bias and cognitive agency surrender in human-AI interaction.
rationale: Addresses human oversight and cognitive load in the context of AI agents, proposing a systems-level intervention to improve human-agent collaboration and prevent cognitive agency surrender.

title: Cognitively Diverse Multiple-Choice Question Generation: A Hybrid Multi-Agent Framework with Large Language Models
summary: ReQUESTA is a hybrid multi-agent framework that decomposes MCQ authoring into specialized subtasks and coordinates LLM agents with rule-based components to improve the reliability and controllability of cognitively diverse MCQ generation.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent framework that uses coordination and workflow design to improve the reliability and controllability of LLM-based generation, which is a systems-engineering contribution.

title: ColPackAgent: Agent-Skill-Guided Hard-Particle Monte Carlo Workflows for Colloidal Packing
summary: ColPackAgent is an agent framework that uses a Model Context Protocol (MCP) tool server to expose a domain-specific Python package for colloidal packing simulations, enabling reliable execution of complex scientific workflows by LLM agents.
rationale: The paper describes a system for reliably executing scientific workflows by integrating domain-specific tools via an MCP server and agent skills, addressing a key challenge in agentic systems' tool use.

title: CoMAI: A Collaborative Multi-Agent Framework for Robust and Equitable Interview Evaluation
summary: CoMAI is a multi-agent framework for interview evaluation that uses a modular task-decomposition architecture and a centralized finite-state machine to coordinate specialized agents for question generation, security, scoring, and summarization, providing multi-layered security defenses and reducing subjective bias.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent system with a specific coordination architecture and explicit security defenses against prompt injection, addressing trustworthiness at the system level.

title: CommitDistill: A Lightweight Knowledge-Centric Memory Layer for Software Repositories
summary: CommitDistill is a lightweight, deterministic, and local-only memory layer that extracts typed knowledge units from software repository commit history using regex and surfaces them via TF-IDF retrieval, providing an inspectable memory substrate for AI coding assistants.
rationale: The paper contributes a concrete, trust-instrumented memory system for agents operating on software repositories, focusing on reliability and inspectability of the memory layer.

title: COMPASS: The explainable agentic framework for Sovereignty, Sustainability, Compliance, and Ethics
summary: COMPASS is a multi-agent orchestration framework that integrates specialized sub-agents for sovereignty, sustainability, compliance, and ethics, using RAG and LLM-as-a-judge to enforce value-aligned AI and provide explainable justifications.
rationale: The paper proposes a multi-agent orchestration system with explicit governance mechanisms for critical trustworthiness properties, addressing a systems-level problem.

title: Compiling Agentic Workflows into LLM Weights: Near-Frontier Quality at Two Orders of Magnitude Less Cost
summary: Proposes compiling agentic workflows directly into fine-tuned LLM weights to reduce cost and improve privacy compared to external orchestration, empirically addressing perceived barriers across three complex use cases.
rationale: Addresses a fundamental architectural choice for agentic systems with implications for cost, privacy, and reliability, and provides empirical validation.

title: ConMem: Structured Memory-Guided Adaptation in Training-Free Multi-Agent Systems
summary: ConMem is a training-free framework that distills historical multi-agent interaction trajectories into a relation-aware memory graph to provide structured guidance for robust and efficient adaptation.
rationale: The paper contributes to agent memory management by proposing a structured, relation-aware memory graph for multi-agent adaptation, improving efficiency and robustness without additional training.

title: Consensus is Strategically Insufficient: Reasoning-Trace Disagreement as a Knowledge-Representation Signal
summary: Proposes a knowledge-representation layer that abstracts reasoning traces and agent decisions into symbolic disagreement states to enable disagreement-aware strategic routing in multi-agent systems, particularly for value-laden tasks.
rationale: Addresses multi-agent coordination and routing based on reasoning-trace analysis, which is a systems-level concern for trustworthy agentic systems.

title: Context Engineering: From Prompts to Corporate Multi-Agent Architecture
summary: Introduces context engineering as a discipline for designing and managing the informational environment of AI agents, proposing five context quality criteria and framing it as the agent's operating system, with higher-order disciplines of intent and specification engineering.
rationale: This paper provides a foundational framework and taxonomy for engineering trustworthy agentic systems, addressing the full operational stack beyond individual prompts.

title: Cooperation Breakdown in LLM Agents Under Communication Delays
summary: Investigates the impact of communication delays on cooperation breakdown in LLM-based multi-agent systems using a Continuous Prisoner's Dilemma simulation, revealing a U-shaped relationship between delay and mutual cooperation.
rationale: The paper directly addresses a systems-level factor (communication delay) affecting coordination and cooperation in multi-agent systems, which is relevant to their reliability.

title: Counterfactual Reasoning for Causal Responsibility Attribution in Probabilistic Multi-Agent Systems
summary: Introduces a formal framework for retrospective counterfactual responsibility attribution in probabilistic multi-agent systems, using Shapley values to quantify agent accountability for outcomes in concurrent stochastic games.
rationale: Addresses responsibility allocation in multi-agent systems, which is relevant to human oversight and governance of agent actions, and provides a formal method for attribution.

title: CounterRefine: Answer-Conditioned Counterevidence Retrieval for Inference-Time Knowledge Repair in Factual Question Answering
summary: CounterRefine is a lightweight repair layer for RAG that uses answer-conditioned counterevidence retrieval and a guarded refinement step to improve factual question answering by reconsidering and repairing initial answers.
rationale: The paper contributes to the reliability of RAG-backed agents by introducing a mechanism for self-correction and knowledge repair within the memory and reasoning process.

title: Critique of Agent Model
summary: A conceptual framework for distinguishing agentic systems (engineered workflows) from agentive systems (endogenous capabilities) based on five dimensions, proposing the Goal-Identity-Configurator (GIC) architecture for general-purpose agents.
rationale: This paper provides a foundational conceptual framework for understanding agency in AI systems, which is crucial for engineering trustworthy agentic systems, and discusses auditability and safety.

title: Cryptographic certificates of validity for trustworthy AI
summary: Proposes cryptographic certificates of validity for agentic AI systems, translating formal policy conditions into succinct cryptographic proofs to certify agent actions without re-execution or trust in the agent.
rationale: Introduces a foundational cryptographic mechanism for certifying agent actions against formal policies, addressing a core trustworthiness problem at the system level.

title: Cutscene Agent: An LLM Agent Framework for Automated 3D Cutscene Generation
summary: Cutscene Agent introduces a bidirectional Model Context Protocol (MCP) for LLM agents to interact with a game engine, enabling closed-loop generation of cinematic assets and addressing long-horizon tool orchestration challenges.
rationale: The paper contributes to tool-interface design with bidirectional integration and addresses long-horizon, multi-step orchestration, which is relevant to agentic systems reliability.

title: CVEvolve: Autonomous Algorithm Discovery for Unstructured Scientific Data Processing
summary: CVEvolve is an autonomous agentic harness that discovers and improves scientific data-processing algorithms using a multi-round search strategy, code execution, evaluation, and holdout testing, demonstrating a reference architecture for zero-code algorithm development.
rationale: This paper describes an end-to-end agentic system for algorithm discovery, including execution, evaluation, and testing components, which is a relevant architecture for trustworthy agents.

title: Data-Centric Interpretability for LLM-based Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
summary: Applies Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) and LLM-summarizer methods to interpret training dynamics and emergent behaviors in LLM-based multi-agent reinforcement learning, identifying fine-grained behaviors and reward hacking.
rationale: The paper contributes to understanding and interpreting agent behavior during training, which is crucial for ensuring reliability and safety in agentic systems, aligning with observation and evaluation concerns.

title: DE-FIVE: Detecting Malicious Image Prompts via Fourier Features and Image Vector Embeddings
summary: DE-FIVE is a training-free framework that detects malicious image prompts, including indirect prompt injections, by analyzing Fourier features and image vector embeddings to protect VLMs from adversarial attacks.
rationale: The paper addresses a specific red-teaming problem (malicious image prompts and indirect prompt injection) in vision-language models, which are components of agentic systems, and proposes a concrete detection mechanism.

title: Decentralized Multi-Agent Systems with Shared Context
summary: DeLM is a decentralized multi-agent system framework that uses a shared verified context and task queue to enable asynchronous coordination and reduce communication bottlenecks in large-scale reasoning tasks.
rationale: The paper proposes a decentralized coordination mechanism for multi-agent systems, addressing a systems-level bottleneck in agent orchestration.

title: Decentralized Ranking Aggregation via Gossip: Convergence and Robustness
summary: Proposes a decentralized ranking aggregation method using gossip algorithms for multi-agent systems, focusing on convergence and robustness to corrupted nodes in a distributed setting.
rationale: Addresses decentralized consensus and robustness in multi-agent systems, which is a core coordination and reliability problem for trustworthy agents.

title: DeepBD: A Grounded Agentic Workflow for Variant Prioritization and Diagnosis of Genetic Birth Defects
summary: DeepBD is a grounded agentic workflow for genetic variant prioritization and diagnosis, separating evidence integration, tool-based refinement, and LLM-assisted diagnostic review to improve recall on birth defect cases.
rationale: This paper presents a reference architecture for a trustworthy agentic system in a high-stakes domain, detailing the workflow and its components for reliability and safety.

title: DeepRoot: A KG-Coordinated Multi-Agent System for Therapeutic Reasoning over Historical Medical Texts
summary: DeepRoot is a multi-agent LLM system that coordinates knowledge graph construction and utilization for therapeutic reasoning over historical medical texts, demonstrating improved reasoning quality and reduced hallucination compared to baseline LLMs.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent system that coordinates knowledge graph building and reasoning to improve reliability and safety in a complex domain, addressing a systems-level challenge in agentic design.

title: DeepSeek Robustness Against Semantic-Character Dual-Space Mutated Prompt Injection
summary: Proposes PromptFuzz-SC, a semantic-character dual-space mutation framework for evaluating LLM robustness against prompt injection, integrating semantic transformations with character-level obfuscation to discover adversarial prompts.
rationale: This paper contributes to red-teaming by proposing a novel dual-space prompt injection attack framework, which is relevant for evaluating the security of agentic systems.

title: Design Principles for Human-Agent Interaction
summary: This position paper proposes 14 design principles for human-agent interaction across four stages, aiming to guide the systematic design and evaluation of usable, trustworthy, and effective agents in real-world settings.
rationale: This paper provides foundational design principles for human-agent interaction, directly addressing the trustworthiness and usability of agentic systems from a human-centric perspective.

title: Detection of adversarial intent in Human-AI teams using LLMs
summary: Investigates the use of LLMs as defensive supervisors to detect malicious behavior in human-AI team interactions from conversation traces, demonstrating their capability for real-time, task-agnostic detection.
rationale: The paper proposes using LLMs for real-time detection of adversarial intent within human-AI team interactions, contributing to human oversight and security of agentic systems.

title: Diagnosing CFG Interpretation in LLMs
summary: A framework for evaluating LLMs' ability to interpret context-free grammars in agentic systems, revealing hierarchical degradation in semantic understanding under structural complexity.
rationale: This paper provides foundational insights into LLM limitations as in-context interpreters for agentic systems, directly impacting the reliability of dynamically defined interfaces.

title: DiagramNet: An End-to-End Recognition Framework and Dataset for Non-Standard System-Level Diagrams
summary: A multi-agent workflow for decomposing complex visual reasoning tasks on system-level diagrams into Perception, Reasoning, and Knowledge stages, demonstrating improved performance and generalization for MLLMs.
rationale: The paper proposes a decoupled multi-agent workflow for complex visual reasoning, which is a contribution to agent coordination and reliability, with a focus on system-level diagrams.

title: Differentiable Modal Logic for Multi-Agent Diagnosis, Orchestration and Communication
summary: Introduces Differentiable Modal Logic (DML) and Modal Logical Neural Networks (MLNNs) to learn and reason about trust, causality, and permissions in multi-agent systems for debugging, orchestration, and communication.
rationale: This paper contributes a neurosymbolic framework for learning and applying modal logic to diagnose and control multi-agent system behavior, directly addressing coordination and reliability concerns.

title: Directional Embedding Smoothing for Robust Vision Language Models
summary: Extends the Randomized Embedding Smoothing and Token Aggregation (RESTA) defense to Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to mitigate multi-modal jailbreaking attacks at inference time.
rationale: Addresses a specific security vulnerability (jailbreaking) in a core component (VLM) of agentic systems with an inference-time defense, fitting the execution-shell layer.

title: Disentangling Adversarial Prompts: A Semantic-Graph Defense for Robust LLM Security
summary: The Adversarial Prompt Disentanglement (APD) framework uses semantic decomposition and graph-based intent classification to identify and neutralize malicious components in adversarial prompts before LLM processing.
rationale: The paper proposes a defense mechanism against adversarial prompts, which is a direct contribution to red-teaming and security of LLM systems.

title: Dissecting model behavior through agent trajectories
summary: Formalizes the 'intent-execution' gap in agentic systems and uses a custom harness to analyze 138k trajectories, revealing model-level differences in problem-solving behavior through finer-grained metrics like edit frequency and testing activity.
rationale: The paper directly addresses the systems problem of agent performance by analyzing agent trajectories and proposing metrics for understanding model behavior within a harness, contributing to observability and evaluation.

title: DocSync: Agentic Documentation Maintenance via Critic-Guided Reflexion
summary: DocSync is an agentic workflow that uses AST representations and RAG for dependency-aware context, combined with a critic-guided refinement loop, to maintain semantic consistency between code and documentation.
rationale: The paper describes an agentic workflow with a critic-guided refinement loop for a specific software engineering task, contributing to the reliability of agentic systems in maintaining code integrity.

title: Dynamic Coordination Strategy Selection for Enterprise Multi-Agent Systems
summary: Evaluates dynamic coordination strategy selection for enterprise multi-agent systems across various tasks and models, finding that dynamic routing can achieve near-best performance compared to fixed strategies.
rationale: The paper directly addresses the operational challenge of selecting coordination strategies in multi-agent systems, contributing to the reliability of agent workflows.

title: DynaSchedBench: Calibrated Dynamic Scheduling Benchmarks and Observability Paradox in LLM-based Scheduling Agents
summary: DynaSchedBench is a diagnostic framework for dynamic scheduling that uses a Sequential Event-Space Calibrator to stratify instances by difficulty, enabling rigorous testing and evaluation of LLM-based scheduling agents.
rationale: The paper introduces a calibrated evaluation framework for LLM agents in a complex dynamic environment, identifying an 'Observability Paradox' and limitations of LLM agents, which is relevant to long-horizon evaluation and reliability.

title: EAA: Automating materials characterization with vision language model agents
summary: EAA is a vision-language-model-driven agentic system with a flexible task-manager architecture and a tool ecosystem for automating complex experimental microscopy workflows, demonstrated at an imaging beamline.
rationale: This paper presents an end-to-end agentic system architecture for automating scientific experiments, including tool integration and workflow management, which is relevant to trustworthy agentic systems.

title: EGTR-Review: Efficient Evidence-Grounded Scientific Peer Review Generation via Multi-Agent Teacher Distillation
summary: EGTR-Review proposes a multi-agent teacher framework for scientific peer review generation that performs structured decomposition, evidence retrieval, and verification, then distills these reasoning trajectories into a lightweight student model for efficient, evidence-grounded review generation.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent system for a complex task, focusing on the coordination of agents for evidence-grounded reasoning and then distilling this process into a more efficient system, which contributes to the reliability of agentic workflows.

title: Embodied-BenchClaw: An Autonomous Multi-Agent System for Embodied Spatial Intelligence Benchmark Construction
summary: Embodied-BenchClaw is an autonomous multi-agent system that constructs and maintains embodied spatial intelligence benchmarks through a five-stage pipeline coordinated by planning, construction, and evaluation agents, enhancing reusability and reliability via a Skill Library and quality control.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent system for an engineering task (benchmark construction) with a focus on reliability, reusability, and maintainability, which are systems-level concerns.

title: EngiAI: A Multi-Agent Framework and Benchmark Suite for LLM-Driven Engineering Design
summary: Introduces a multi-agent framework and benchmark suite, EngiBench, for LLM-driven engineering design, evaluating workflow complexity, RAG contributions, and HPC orchestration, revealing challenges in conditional branching and long-running multi-step instruction following.
rationale: The paper provides a benchmark suite and reference implementation for evaluating multi-agent systems on complex engineering workflows, including tool use, RAG, and HPC orchestration, which directly contributes to observation and evaluation of agent reliability.

title: Enhancing Multi-Agent Communication through Attention Steering with Context Relevance
summary: Agent-Radar is a training-free context management method that dynamically steers agent attention to relevant context using temporal and spatial decay, improving performance in long multi-agent conversations.
rationale: The paper addresses a systems-level problem of context management and information dilution in multi-agent memory, directly impacting reliability.

title: Entropy-Based Observability for AI Agent Behavior
summary: Proposes Entropy-Based Observability for AI Agents (EOA), a lightweight framework for deriving behavioral telemetry from agent traces to provide deeper insights into internal agent behavior beyond outcome-oriented metrics.
rationale: Directly addresses the observability of agent behavior by proposing a framework for deriving internal behavioral telemetry from execution traces.

title: Entropy Guided Diversification and Preference Elicitation in Agentic Recommendation Systems
summary: An Interactive Decision Support System (IDSS) uses entropy to guide adaptive preference elicitation and uncertainty-aware ranking, improving interaction efficiency and recommendation quality under ambiguous user intent.
rationale: The paper addresses the challenge of ambiguous user intent in agentic recommendation systems by using entropy-guided preference elicitation and uncertainty-aware ranking, which is a direct contribution to the 'intent' harness layer.

title: Evaluating Cooperation in LLM Social Groups through Elected Leadership
summary: Investigates how elected leadership and election mechanisms can improve cooperation and social welfare in multi-agent LLM systems, demonstrating significant gains in social welfare and survival time.
rationale: The paper directly addresses multi-agent coordination and governance mechanisms, which are critical for the reliability and safety of agentic systems.

title: Evolve as a Team: Collaborative Self-Evolution for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
summary: Meta-Team is an experience-driven framework for multi-agent system evolution that enables collaborative self-evolution by preserving execution context and coordinating post-task communication to improve agent behaviors, inter-agent coordination, and team organization.
rationale: The paper addresses the reliability of multi-agent systems by proposing a framework for collaborative self-evolution based on execution experience, which is a systems-level contribution to agent coordination.

title: EvoMAS: Learning Execution-Time Workflows for Multi-Agent Systems
summary: EvoMAS is a framework that learns to adapt multi-agent workflows at execution time based on evolving task states, addressing the limitations of static coordination strategies for long-horizon tasks.
rationale: The paper contributes to the dynamic coordination and reliability of multi-agent systems by learning execution-time workflow adaptation, which is a systems-level concern.

title: Examining Agents' Bias Amplification versus Suppression in Multi-Agent Systems
summary: This paper empirically examines how individual agent biases, when exposed uniformly, can amplify system-wide fairness issues in multi-agent systems, proposing a metric to quantify this shift.
rationale: The paper investigates bias amplification in multi-agent systems, which is a system-level safety concern related to agent coordination and interaction.

title: ExecTune: Effective Steering of Black-Box LLMs with Guide Models
summary: Introduces Guide-Core Policies (GCoP) as an abstraction for agentic systems where a guide model generates a strategy for a black-box core model, and proposes ExecTune to optimize guide-averaged executability for improved accuracy and cost efficiency.
rationale: The paper formalizes and optimizes the coordination between a guide and core model in agentic systems, directly addressing reliability and efficiency in multi-model orchestration.

title: Executable World Models for ARC-AGI-3 in the Era of Coding Agents
summary: An agent system for ARC-AGI-3 that uses an executable Python world model, verifies it against observations, and plans through it, with an audit of information channels and mitigation of leakage in the harness.
rationale: The paper describes an agent architecture with a focus on the system's design, including an audit of information channels and mitigation of leakage, which is relevant to trustworthy agentic systems.

title: Explicit Trait Inference for Multi-Agent Coordination
summary: Explicit Trait Inference (ETI) enables LLM agents to infer and track partner characteristics (warmth, competence) from interaction histories to improve coordination and reduce failures in multi-agent systems.
rationale: The paper directly addresses coordination failures in multi-agent systems by introducing a mechanism for agents to infer and leverage partner traits, contributing to the reliability of agentic workflows.

title: FileGram: Grounding Agent Personalization in File-System Behavioral Traces
summary: FileGram is a framework for grounding agent memory and personalization in file-system behavioral traces, including a data engine for simulation, a benchmark for evaluation, and a memory architecture that builds user profiles from atomic actions and content deltas.
rationale: The paper proposes a memory architecture and evaluation framework for agents operating on local file systems, directly addressing memory management and personalization from a systems perspective.

title: Finding the Weakest Link: Adversarial Attack against Multi-Agent Communications
summary: Investigates single-victim communication perturbation attacks against multi-agent systems by using gradient information to identify susceptible messages, agents, and timesteps, and proposes adversarial loss functions to enhance attack effectiveness.
rationale: The paper directly addresses adversarial attacks on multi-agent communication, which is a system-level security concern for agentic systems.

title: First, Do No Harm (With LLMs): Mitigating Racial Bias via Agentic Workflows
summary: Evaluates racial bias in LLMs for clinical settings using EU AI Act guidelines and demonstrates that retrieval-based agentic workflows can mitigate some explicit bias in differential diagnosis tasks.
rationale: The paper investigates bias mitigation through agentic workflows, specifically using retrieval, which is a systems-level approach to improving trustworthiness.

title: Five Fatal Assumptions: Why T-Shirt Sizing Systematically Fails for AI Projects
summary: Analyzes five foundational assumptions of traditional software estimation that systematically fail in AI projects, particularly for LLMs and multi-agent systems, and proposes a 'Checkpoint Sizing' approach for iterative reassessment.
rationale: Provides a foundational analysis of why traditional project management assumptions fail for AI agentic systems, contributing to understanding reliability challenges in their development.

title: ForecastCompass: Guiding Agentic Forecasting with Adaptive Factor Memory
summary: ForecastCompass (FoCo) is an adaptive factor-based memory framework that organizes forecasting experience with a hierarchical taxonomy and maintains factor and reasoning memory components, iteratively revised through retrospective analyses to improve probabilistic accuracy and calibration in agentic forecasting.
rationale: The paper proposes a novel memory framework specifically designed for agentic forecasting, addressing how agents learn and store reusable predictive factors and calibration knowledge, which is a systems-level concern for agent memory.

title: FORGE: Self-Evolving Agent Memory With No Weight Updates via Population Broadcast
summary: FORGE is a population-based protocol that evolves prompt-injected natural-language memory (heuristics and few-shot examples) for hierarchical ReAct agents, improving decision-making and reducing failure rates in a stochastic network-defense environment.
rationale: The paper contributes to agent memory management by evolving self-generated knowledge artifacts to improve agent reliability in long-horizon, stochastic environments.

title: FormalScience: Scalable Human-in-the-Loop Autoformalisation of Science with Agentic Code Generation in Lean
summary: FormalScience is a human-in-the-loop agentic pipeline that enables domain experts to produce syntactically correct and semantically aligned formal proofs from informal scientific reasoning, addressing the challenge of autoformalisation in scientific domains.
rationale: The paper describes a human-in-the-loop agentic pipeline for autoformalisation, which directly contributes to the oversight layer by enabling domain experts to guide and verify agent output for reliability.

title: Foundation-Model-Based Agents in Industrial Automation: Purposes, Capabilities, and Open Challenges
summary: A systematic literature survey on foundation-model-based agents in industrial automation, identifying their purposes, capabilities, and limitations, and proposing a working definition.
rationale: This paper provides a foundational survey and taxonomy of agentic systems in industrial contexts, aligning with the 'foundations' layer by framing the problem and identifying challenges.

title: From Agent-Only Social Networks to Autonomous Scientific Research: Lessons from OpenClaw and Moltbook, and the Architecture of ClawdLab and Beach.Science
summary: Presents two complementary platforms, ClawdLab and Beach.science, for autonomous scientific research, addressing architectural failure modes of prior agent-only social networks through structured collaboration, governance, external tool verification, and a composable third-tier architecture.
rationale: This paper provides architectural designs and lessons learned from real-world agent interactions, directly contributing to the engineering of trustworthy agentic systems through governance, verification, and structured collaboration.

title: From Code-Centric to Intent-Centric Software Engineering: A Reflexive Thematic Analysis of Generative AI, Agentic Systems, and Engineering Accountability
summary: A reflexive thematic analysis of public discourse and literature on generative AI and agentic systems, framing the shift in software engineering from code-centric to intent-centric work and highlighting the increased importance of intent specification, governance, and accountability.
rationale: This paper provides a foundational analysis of the shift to intent-centric software engineering with agentic systems, directly addressing the framing of trustworthiness and accountability as systems problems.

title: From History to State: Constant-Context Skill Learning for LLM Agents
summary: A context-to-weights framework for agent skill learning that moves procedural context from prompts into model weights, reducing prompt tokens and improving privacy by minimizing sensitive context exposure to external APIs.
rationale: Addresses privacy and cost concerns in agent memory management by internalizing recurring workflow context into model weights, a systems-level optimization for trustworthy agents.

title: From Intent to AI Pipelines: A Controlled Agentic Framework for Non-AI Expert Scientists
summary: A human-in-the-loop agentic framework (DDAP) that guides non-AI expert scientists through a structured, four-stage process to systematically construct AI pipelines and their implementation code, maintaining user control over key decisions.
rationale: The paper describes a controlled agentic framework with human-in-the-loop oversight for generating AI pipelines, addressing the challenge of non-expert users and maintaining control over the process.

title: From Intent to Execution: Composing Agentic Workflows with Agent Recommendation
summary: A framework for automated multi-agent system creation, including an LLM-derived planner, dynamic call graph, orchestrator, and a two-stage agent recommender with a critique agent for comprehensive review and revision of task-based agent selection.
rationale: The paper contributes to the automated composition and orchestration of multi-agent systems, including a critique agent for reliability, which is a systems-engineering concern.

title: From Prompt to Service: An SLM-Based Agent Orchestration Gateway for AI-Driven Virtual Worlds
summary: An SLM-based Agent Orchestration Gateway decouples virtual world clients from heterogeneous AI backends by using an edge-deployed SLM for intent-driven service routing, improving extensibility and coordination of distributed services.
rationale: The paper presents a concrete architectural solution for orchestrating AI services in a distributed agentic system, addressing reliability and extensibility concerns.

title: From Static Analysis to Audience Dissemination: A Training-Free Multimodal Controversy Detection Multi-Agent Framework
summary: A training-free multi-agent framework, AuDisAgent, for multimodal controversy detection that models audience dissemination through specialized screening agents, a viewing panel agent for discussion, and an arbitration agent for final judgment.
rationale: The paper proposes a multi-agent framework for controversy detection, explicitly modeling coordination and decision-making among agents to address a safety-critical task.

title: From Storage to Experience: A Survey on the Evolution of LLM Agent Memory Mechanisms
summary: This survey proposes an evolutionary framework for LLM agent memory mechanisms, formalizing development into Storage, Reflection, and Experience stages, and exploring proactive exploration and cross-trajectory abstraction for long-range consistency and continual learning.
rationale: The paper provides a foundational survey and framework for agent memory mechanisms, directly addressing a core component of trustworthy agentic systems.

title: From Task-Guided Conversational Graphs to Goal-Oriented Dialogue Runtimes
summary: Introduces the Goal-Oriented Dialogue Runtime (GODR), a framework-neutral design pattern for managing complex, multi-domain, interruptible conversations by treating goals, task frames, and lifecycle state as first-class runtime objects to ensure objective continuity.
rationale: This paper proposes a systems-level design pattern for managing complex conversational state and goal continuity in multi-agent orchestration, directly addressing reliability in complex agentic workflows.

title: From Threat Intelligence to Firewall Rules: Semantic Relations in Hybrid AI Agent and Expert System Architectures
summary: A neuro-symbolic multi-agent system that leverages hypernym-hyponym semantic relations to extract threat intelligence and automatically generate CLIPS code for firewall rules.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent system that automates the generation of security controls (firewall rules) from threat intelligence, addressing a critical security and coordination problem in agentic systems.

title: Fully Homomorphic Encryption on Llama 3 model for privacy preserving LLM inference
summary: Integrates Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) into the LLM inference pipeline to protect sensitive data and model parameters against privacy attacks, including those from quantum computing.
rationale: Addresses privacy and security concerns within the LLM operational stack by applying FHE to the inference pipeline, which is relevant to memory and execution-shell integrity.

title: GAM: Hierarchical Graph-based Agentic Memory for LLM Agents
summary: GAM proposes a hierarchical graph-based memory framework that decouples memory encoding from consolidation to improve long-term consistency and reduce interference from transient noise in LLM agents.
rationale: The paper directly addresses memory management and integrity for long-running agents, focusing on reliability and consistency of knowledge retention.

title: Generated, Parallel, Scalable? A Study of Agentic AI-Generated Julia Code on Supercomputers
summary: Evaluates agentic generation of parallel Julia code for HPC, identifying reliability issues like deadlocks and out-of-memory errors at scale, and comparing different LLMs and parallelization frameworks.
rationale: This paper studies the reliability of AI-generated code in a specific, high-stakes domain (HPC) and identifies systemic failures in agentic workflows, contributing to understanding agentic system limitations.

title: GoAgent: Group-of-Agents Communication Topology Generation for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
summary: GoAgent proposes a communication topology generation method for multi-agent systems that explicitly models collaborative groups as atomic units, aiming to improve coordination and reduce communication overhead.
rationale: The paper directly addresses multi-agent coordination and communication efficiency, which are systems-level concerns for trustworthy agentic systems.

title: Goal-Oriented Reasoning for RAG-based Memory in Conversational Agentic LLM Systems
summary: Goal-Mem is a goal-oriented reasoning framework for RAG-based agentic memory that uses backward chaining and subgoal decomposition to improve retrieval and reasoning over long-horizon conversations, particularly for multi-hop questions.
rationale: The paper addresses a systems-level problem of memory retrieval and reasoning for long-horizon agentic behavior, improving reliability of RAG-based memory systems.

title: Graph-based Agent Memory: Taxonomy, Techniques, and Applications
summary: A comprehensive survey and taxonomy of graph-based agent memory, analyzing techniques for extraction, storage, retrieval, and evolution to improve memory system reliability.
rationale: This paper provides a foundational survey of graph-based agent memory, directly addressing the 'memory' harness layer and aiming for more reliable memory systems.

title: Graph-based Target Back-Propagation for Context Adaptation in Multi-LLM Agentic Systems
summary: A context adaptation framework for multi-LLM agentic workflows that uses graph-based target back-propagation to iteratively revise prompts based on task feedback, addressing credit assignment and convergence.
rationale: The paper contributes to the reliability of multi-agent coordination by providing a method for context adaptation and prompt optimization within agentic workflows, with theoretical convergence guarantees.

title: Guided Collaboration in Heterogeneous LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems via Entropy-Based Understanding Assessment and Experience Retrieval
summary: Proposes an Entropy-Based Adaptive Guidance Framework to dynamically align guidance with the cognitive state of agents in heterogeneous multi-agent systems, using multi-dimensional entropy metrics and RAG for experience retrieval to improve collaboration effectiveness and stability.
rationale: Addresses a systems-level problem in multi-agent coordination by proposing a framework for adaptive guidance and experience retrieval to improve reliability in heterogeneous agent collaborations.

title: HAGE: Harnessing Agentic Memory via RL-Driven Weighted Graph Evolution
summary: HAGE proposes a weighted multi-relational memory framework that uses an RL-driven graph evolution to improve query-conditioned memory traversal, enhancing long-horizon reasoning accuracy.
rationale: The paper contributes to agent memory systems by introducing a dynamic, learned approach to memory retrieval, addressing reliability in long-horizon reasoning.

title: HAIF: A Human-AI Integration Framework for Hybrid Team Operations
summary: Proposes the Human-AI Integration Framework (HAIF) as a protocol-based operational system for organizing daily work in hybrid human-AI teams, focusing on delegation, tiered autonomy, and feedback mechanisms.
rationale: Addresses human oversight and integration challenges for agentic systems in operational workflows, which is a core concern for trustworthy agentic systems.

title: Harnessing Agentic Evolution
summary: AEvo is a meta-editing framework that allows a meta-agent to observe the accumulated evolution context and edit the procedure or agent context controlling future evolution, improving long-horizon search stability and performance.
rationale: The paper introduces a meta-agent framework for steering and stabilizing long-horizon agentic evolution, addressing a systems-level challenge in multi-step agent coordination and reliability.

title: Harnessing Generalist Agents for Contextualized Time Series
summary: TimeClaw is an agentic harness framework that provides generalist LLM agents with time series-native runtime support, including executable temporal tools, experience-driven capability evolution, and episodic multimodal memory for contextualized temporal reasoning.
rationale: The paper describes an agentic harness framework with specific systems components (tools, memory, runtime support) for a specialized domain, contributing to agent architectures.

title: Herding CATs: ALARA for Agent Harness Engineering in Portable Composable Multi-Agent Teams
summary: Introduces a plain-text context-agent-tool (CAT) data layer for declaratively managing tool access and modifications for agents, enabling more scalable and verifiable control over agent behavior.
rationale: Addresses a systems-engineering problem in managing agent tool access and configuration, with a concrete data layer and evaluation.

title: Heterogeneous Debate Engine: Identity-Grounded Cognitive Architecture for Resilient LLM-Based Ethical Tutoring
summary: A cognitive architecture, Heterogeneous Debate Engine (HDE), combines Identity-Grounded Retrieval-Augmented Generation (ID-RAG) and Heuristic Theory of Mind to maintain doctrinal fidelity and prevent semantic drift in multi-agent ethical tutoring systems.
rationale: The paper addresses the reliability and safety of multi-agent systems by proposing an architectural solution to prevent semantic drift and logical deterioration in dialectical interactions, which is a systems-level concern for agent coordination.

title: Hive: A Multi-Agent Infrastructure for Algorithm- and Task-Level Scaling
summary: Hive is a multi-agent infrastructure that optimizes resource allocation and computation reuse across multiple reasoning branches and agents, addressing algorithm- and task-level scaling for agentic systems.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent infrastructure with specific mechanisms for optimizing resource allocation and computation reuse, which directly contributes to the reliability and efficiency of agent coordination.

title: Hot Fixing in the Wild
summary: Empirical analysis of hot fix patterns in over 61,000 GitHub repositories, comparing human and AI-agent behaviors to understand urgent maintenance workflows and inform future human-automation collaboration.
rationale: The paper provides an empirical analysis of hot-fixing workflows, including AI-agent contributions, which directly informs human oversight and collaboration in urgent maintenance scenarios.

title: How Generation Architecture Shapes Code Complexity in Multi-Agent LLM Systems: A Paired Study on HumanEval
summary: Empirical study of how multi-agent orchestration architectures affect the structural complexity of generated code, identifying specific layers (analyst-coder split, tester) that inflate complexity without corresponding pass@1 advantages.
rationale: This paper investigates the system-level impact of multi-agent coordination architectures on code complexity, a reliability concern, rather than just model capabilities.

title: How Task Structure Limits Multi-Agent Success: An Information-Theoretic Analysis
summary: Information-theoretic analysis showing that multi-agent system success probability is exponentially limited by the information bottleneck arising from task constraint graph partitioning, validated on synthetic and real-world data.
rationale: Provides a foundational understanding of the inherent limitations in multi-agent coordination based on task structure, which is critical for designing reliable agentic systems.

title: HR-Agents: Using Multiple LLM-based Agents to Improve Q&A about Brazilian Labor Legislation
authors: Vitor G. C. B. de Farias
Fabiana C. Q. de O. Marucci
Maria L. A. de S. Cruvinel
summary: A multi-agent system using specialized LLM agents and RAG is introduced to improve the accuracy and efficiency of Q&A about Brazilian labor legislation, with cooperative agent interactions for response validation.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent system with cooperative agent interactions for response validation, which is a contribution to agent coordination and reliability.

title: Human-Guided Agentic AI for Multimodal Clinical Prediction: Lessons from the AgentDS Healthcare Benchmark
summary: Investigates human guidance in agentic AI workflows for multimodal clinical prediction, demonstrating how human intervention at key decision points improves performance and distills lessons for deploying agents in healthcare.
rationale: The paper directly addresses human oversight in agentic systems, specifically in a high-stakes domain like healthcare, and provides practical lessons for deployment.

title: Humans Integrate, Agents Fix: How Agent-Authored Pull Requests Are Referenced in Practice
summary: Empirical study of human-agent and agent-agent interactions in code review, showing how agent-authored pull requests are referenced and integrated, highlighting increased coordination effort and longer review times.
rationale: The paper provides an empirical study of human-agent interaction and coordination in code review, which is relevant to human oversight and the operational challenges of integrating agentic systems into workflows.

title: Improving Role Consistency in Multi-Agent Collaboration via Quantitative Role Clarity
summary: Proposes a quantitative role clarity metric based on semantic similarity between agent behavior and role descriptions, used as a regularizer during fine-tuning to improve role consistency and task performance in multi-agent systems.
rationale: Addresses a specific failure mode in multi-agent coordination (role consistency) with a quantitative metric and a method to improve it, demonstrating system-level reliability gains.

title: In harmony with gpt-oss
summary: Reverse-engineers the implicit tool-use behavior of gpt-oss-20b and builds a native agent harness to reproduce published scores, demonstrating the importance of understanding model-native interfaces for reliable agent performance.
rationale: The paper reverse-engineers an agent's implicit tool-use and builds a native harness, contributing to understanding and reproducing agent system behavior.

title: InfoSeeker: A Scalable Hierarchical Parallel Agent Framework for Web Information Seeking
summary: InfoSeeker is a hierarchical parallel agent framework that uses context isolation and aggregation at the Manager layer to prevent context saturation and error propagation in wide-scale information synthesis tasks.
rationale: The paper addresses system-level challenges like context saturation and error propagation in multi-agent coordination for information seeking, contributing to agent reliability.

title: Infrastructure for Valuable, Tradable, and Verifiable Agent Memory
summary: Proposes an infrastructure (clawgang and meowtrade) to bind agent memory to verifiable computational provenance, enabling its transfer and trade as a certified asset in an agent economy.
rationale: Addresses the integrity and provenance of agent memory, enabling its verifiable and tradable use, which is a systems-level concern for trustworthy agents.

title: Insurance of Agentic AI
summary: This paper develops a framework for understanding the emerging insurance market for agentic AI, characterizing novel risk pathways and proposing an actuarial framework and coordinated insurance architecture for managing these exposures.
rationale: The paper provides a foundational framework for understanding and managing risks in agentic AI systems, which is crucial for engineering trustworthy systems, even if it's not a direct technical implementation.

title: Internet of Agentic AI: Incentive-Compatible Distributed Teaming and Workflow
summary: Proposes a framework for distributed, incentive-compatible multi-agent coordination, enabling dynamic coalition formation and workflow execution across heterogeneous cloud and edge infrastructure.
rationale: Addresses scalable coordination and distributed teaming for agentic systems, which is a core systems-engineering challenge for trustworthy agents.

title: Interpretable Context Methodology: Folder Structure as Agentic Architecture
summary: Model Workspace Protocol (MWP) proposes using filesystem structure and markdown files to orchestrate sequential agent workflows, replacing complex multi-agent frameworks with a simpler, human-reviewable architecture.
rationale: The paper introduces a novel architectural pattern for agent orchestration, focusing on simplifying context management and human oversight for sequential workflows.

title: IPO Finance Agent: Evaluation of LLM Financial Analysts beyond Finance Agent v2, with Automated Rubric Generation -- the Case of the SpaceX (SPCX) IPO
summary: Introduces IPO Finance Agent, an extension of Finance Agent v2, with an improved contextual retrieval architecture to handle long S-1 filings for IPO due diligence, and an automated rubric generation pipeline for evaluation.
rationale: The paper contributes to agentic systems by improving retrieval architecture for long documents and introducing an automated evaluation rubric generation pipeline, addressing reliability in agent performance.

title: Know When to Trust the Skill: Delayed Appraisal and Epistemic Vigilance for Single-Agent LLMs
summary: Introduces MESA-S, a single-agent framework that uses delayed appraisal and epistemic vigilance to separate self-confidence from source-confidence, improving trust provenance and mitigating supply-chain vulnerabilities by decoupling skill utility awareness from execution.
rationale: The paper contributes to agent orchestration by introducing metacognitive governance for single agents, addressing trust provenance and supply-chain vulnerabilities in tool use.

title: KPI2KVI: A Multi Agent Workflow for Calculating Key Value Indicators from Service Descriptions
summary: KPI2KVI is a multi-agent workflow that transforms natural language service descriptions into computed KVI estimates by orchestrating agents for context elicitation, KVI category extraction, KPI generation, value collection, and KVI calculation with traceable explanations.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent workflow for a complex task, focusing on the orchestration logic and providing traceable explanations for auditing, which contributes to the reliability of agentic systems.

title: LAMMI-Pathology: A Tool-Centric Bottom-Up LVLM-Agent Framework for Molecularly Informed Medical Intelligence in Pathology
summary: LAMMI-Pathology is a hierarchical, tool-centric agent framework for pathology that uses domain-adaptive tools, component agents, and a top-level planner, with a novel trajectory construction mechanism for robust inference and tool use.
rationale: The paper describes a hierarchical agent coordination framework with a focus on robust tool use and reasoning trajectories, which contributes to the reliability of agentic systems.

title: Large Language Models Do Not Always Need Readable Language
summary: Investigates compact, non-standard textual representations (BabelTele) for LLM-to-LLM communication and agent memory to reduce context overhead while preserving semantic fidelity.
rationale: The paper explores model-native representations for agent memory and multi-agent communication, directly addressing context overhead and reliability in agentic systems.

title: Large Language Models Exhibit Normative Conformity
summary: Investigates informational and normative conformity in LLMs within multi-agent systems, demonstrating how LLMs can be manipulated by social context and suggesting distinct internal mechanisms for these behaviors.
rationale: This paper directly addresses a vulnerability in multi-agent coordination by analyzing how LLMs conform to group norms, which can be exploited by malicious actors.

title: Latent-DARM: Bridging Discrete Diffusion And Autoregressive Models For Reasoning
summary: Latent-DARM is a latent-space communication framework that bridges discrete diffusion models (for planning) and autoregressive models (for execution) to improve multi-agent collaboration and reasoning accuracy.
rationale: This paper contributes to multi-agent coordination by proposing a novel communication framework that leverages heterogeneous model types for improved reasoning and plan revision.

title: LatentMem: Customizing Latent Memory for Multi-Agent Systems
summary: LatentMem is a learnable multi-agent memory framework that customizes agent-specific memories in a token-efficient manner by synthesizing compact latent memories from an experience bank and agent-specific contexts.
rationale: The paper addresses memory management and efficiency in multi-agent systems, which is a core systems-engineering concern for agent trustworthiness.

title: Learning to Construct Practical Agentic Systems
summary: Proposes a modular agent framework using recursive pseudo-tools and fixed workflows to improve simplicity, controllability, and predictability of inference costs in practical agentic systems, outperforming dynamic planning and hand-engineered agents.
rationale: Addresses practical systems engineering concerns like cost, control, and predictability in agent design, offering a structured architectural approach and optimization methods.

title: Learning to Continually Learn via Meta-learning Agentic Memory Designs
summary: ALMA is a meta-learning framework that automates the design of memory modules for agentic systems, including database schemas and retrieval/update mechanisms, to enable more effective continual learning.
rationale: The paper directly addresses the engineering of agent memory designs for continual learning and adaptation, which is a systems-level concern for agent reliability.

title: Learning to Orchestrate Agents under Uncertainty
summary: BOT-Orch is a lightweight framework that recasts agent orchestration as a bandit problem, regularized by optimal transport distances, to adaptively delegate tasks to heterogeneous agents under uncertainty in their reliability and output distributions.
rationale: This paper directly addresses the coordination and reliability of multi-agent systems by modeling and managing uncertainty in agent behavior at the orchestration layer.

title: Learning to Predict, Discover, and Reason in High-Dimensional Event Sequences
summary: Introduces a multi-agent system to automate the synthesis of Boolean error pattern rules from high-dimensional diagnostic event sequences, addressing the scalability and error-proneness of manual rule creation for vehicle fault diagnostics.
rationale: The paper proposes a multi-agent system for automating the synthesis of diagnostic rules, which directly contributes to the reliability and safety of complex systems by improving the coordination and reasoning capabilities of agents in a high-dimensional event space.

title: Ledger-State Stigmergy: A Formal Framework for Indirect Coordination Grounded in Distributed Ledger State
summary: Introduces a formal framework for indirect coordination of autonomous agents on blockchains, mapping stigmergy to distributed ledger technology and identifying recurring on-chain coordination patterns.
rationale: Provides a formal framework and design guidance for multi-agent coordination using shared ledger state, which is relevant to the reliability of agentic systems.

title: LinguistAgent: A Reflective Multi-Model Platform for Automated Linguistic Annotation
summary: LinguistAgent is a multi-agent platform with an Annotator and Reviewer workflow that simulates peer-review for linguistic annotation, supporting comparative experiments across different LLM paradigms and providing real-time token-level evaluation.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent system with a specific coordination mechanism (annotator/reviewer workflow) and evaluation capabilities for a practical application, fitting the 'coordination' layer and 'reliability' guarantee.

title: LLM-Oriented Information Retrieval: A Denoising-First Perspective
summary: This perspective paper argues that denoising is the primary bottleneck for LLM-oriented information retrieval in RAG and agentic search, proposing a four-stage framework of IR challenges and a taxonomy of signal-to-noise optimization techniques across the information access pipeline.
rationale: The paper directly addresses the problem of noise in RAG and agent memory, which is critical for agent trustworthiness, and provides a systems-level taxonomy of denoising techniques.

title: Loosely-Structured Software: Engineering Context, Structure, and Evolution Entropy in Runtime-Rewired Multi-Agent Systems
summary: Introduces Loosely-Structured Software (LSS) as a new paradigm for engineering multi-agent systems, focusing on managing runtime entropy through View/Context, Structure, and Evolution Engineering principles and design patterns.
rationale: This paper provides a foundational framework and design principles for managing complexity and uncertainty in multi-agent systems, addressing core systems-engineering challenges for agentic software.

title: M$^\star$: Every Task Deserves Its Own Memory Harness
summary: M$^\star$ automatically discovers task-optimized memory harnesses by modeling agent memory as an evolvable Python program, jointly optimizing schema, storage logic, and workflow instructions through reflective code evolution.
rationale: The paper contributes to the engineering of agent memory systems by proposing an automated method for optimizing memory harnesses for specific tasks, which directly impacts the reliability of long-running agents.

title: Machine Learning as a Tool (MLAT): A Framework for Integrating Statistical ML Models as Callable Tools within LLM Agent Workflows
summary: MLAT is a design pattern for integrating pre-trained statistical ML models as callable tools within LLM agent workflows, allowing agents to dynamically invoke quantitative predictions and reason about their outputs.
rationale: The paper introduces a framework for integrating ML models as tools within agent workflows, addressing the tool-interface layer and improving the reliability of agentic systems by enabling dynamic quantitative reasoning.

title: MALLVI: A Multi-Agent Framework for Integrated Generalized Robotics Manipulation
summary: MALLVI is a multi-agent framework for robotic manipulation that coordinates specialized agents (Decomposer, Localizer, Thinker, Reflector) to enable closed-loop feedback-driven execution, improving robustness and generalization in dynamic settings.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent coordination framework for robust robotic manipulation, focusing on closed-loop feedback and error recovery, which directly contributes to the reliability of agentic systems.

title: MAS-H2: A Hierarchical Multi-Agent System for Holistic Cloud-Native Autoscaling
summary: MAS-H2 is a hierarchical multi-agent system that decomposes cloud resource autoscaling into strategic, planning, and execution layers to proactively manage pods and nodes, addressing the strategic void between business policies and resource provisioning.
rationale: This paper presents a multi-agent system for cloud resource management, focusing on coordination and planning to improve reliability and resource efficiency, which is a systems-level contribution.

title: MAS-on-the-Fly: Dynamic Adaptation of LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems at Test Time
summary: MASFly introduces a dynamic adaptation framework for multi-agent systems at test time, using retrieval-augmented SOP instantiation for system generation and an experience-guided Watcher agent for real-time execution supervision.
rationale: The paper describes a system for dynamic adaptation and supervision of multi-agent coordination, addressing reliability and long-horizon evaluation in agentic systems.

title: Mathematics Teachers Interactions with a Multi-Agent System for Personalized Problem Generation
summary: Examines a multi-agent teacher-in-the-loop system for personalizing math problems, focusing on teacher and student interactions and the effectiveness of AI agents in evaluating problem quality.
rationale: The paper investigates human oversight and the effectiveness of multi-agent evaluation in an educational context, which directly relates to the trustworthiness of agentic systems in a real-world application.

title: MAVEN: Improving Generalization in Agentic Tool Calling
summary: MAVEN is a symbolic reasoning scaffold that improves generalization in agentic tool calling through structured decomposition, adaptive tool orchestration, and intermediate verification, evaluated on multi-step reasoning benchmarks.
rationale: The paper contributes to the reliability of agentic systems by improving generalization and coordination in tool calling through a structured reasoning scaffold and process-aware evaluation.

title: mcp-proto-okn: Natural-language access to open scientific knowledge graphs through the Model Context Protocol
summary: A Model Context Protocol server that enables AI assistants to discover, inspect, query, and integrate scientific knowledge graphs through natural language, providing capabilities like graph routing and SPARQL execution.
rationale: This paper describes a tool interface that allows AI agents to interact with external knowledge graphs, which is a direct contribution to the agentic stack.

title: Measuring Biological Capabilities and Risks of AI Agents
summary: This paper provides a framework and practical considerations for designing, running, and interpreting evaluations of AI agents' biological capabilities and risks, focusing on the implicit design choices that shape results.
rationale: The paper contributes to the foundational understanding and evaluation methodology for assessing safety risks in agentic AI systems, particularly in the biological domain.

title: MediHive: A Decentralized Agent Collective for Medical Reasoning
summary: MediHive is a decentralized multi-agent framework for medical question answering that uses a shared memory pool and iterative fusion to achieve consensus, addressing scalability and single points of failure in centralized designs.
rationale: The paper contributes to the coordination layer by proposing a decentralized multi-agent architecture that enhances reliability and fault tolerance for complex reasoning tasks.

title: MedMASLab: A Unified Orchestration Framework for Benchmarking Multimodal Medical Multi-Agent Systems
summary: MedMASLab provides a unified orchestration framework and benchmarking platform for multimodal medical multi-agent systems, addressing architectural fragmentation and standardizing multimodal integration and evaluation.
rationale: The paper introduces a unified framework and benchmarking platform for multi-agent systems, focusing on architectural standardization and evaluation, which is relevant to trustworthy agentic systems.

title: Meissa: Multi-modal Medical Agentic Intelligence
summary: Meissa is a lightweight 4B-parameter medical multi-modal LLM that enables offline agentic capabilities by distilling structured trajectories from frontier models, addressing cost, latency, and privacy concerns for clinical deployment.
rationale: This paper presents an architectural approach for deploying trustworthy medical agents offline, addressing privacy and reliability concerns through distillation of agentic capabilities.

title: MemCog: From Memory-as-Tool to Memory-as-Cognition in Conversational Agents
summary: MemCog introduces a "Memory-as-Cognition" system that integrates memory access into the agent's reasoning process through a navigable memory store and proactive reasoning protocol, addressing limitations of traditional one-shot retrieval.
rationale: The paper proposes a novel memory system architecture that directly impacts how agents access and reason with information, addressing a core systems-engineering aspect of agent memory.

title: MemGraphRAG: Memory-based Multi-Agent System for Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation
summary: MemGraphRAG introduces a memory-based multi-agent system for constructing high-quality knowledge graphs for RAG, ensuring thematic consistency and structural connectivity by providing a unified global context to agents during extraction.
rationale: The paper addresses the reliability of RAG by improving graph construction through a multi-agent system with shared memory, which is a systems-level contribution to agent memory integrity.

title: Memora: A Harmonic Memory Representation Balancing Abstraction and Specificity
summary: Memora introduces a harmonic memory representation that balances abstraction and specificity for agent memory systems, improving retrieval relevance and reasoning effectiveness as memory scales.
rationale: The paper directly addresses the engineering challenge of scaling agent memory while maintaining retrieval effectiveness, a core concern for trustworthy agentic systems.

title: Memory as Ontology: A Constitutional Memory Architecture for Persistent Digital Citizens
summary: Proposes a "Memory-as-Ontology" paradigm and a Constitutional Memory Architecture (CMA) with a four-layer governance hierarchy to ensure identity continuity and persistent existence for long-lived digital agents across model transitions.
rationale: Addresses the fundamental systems problem of memory integrity and identity persistence for long-lived agents, which is crucial for reliability and safety over extended lifecycles.

title: Memory Beyond Recall: A Dual-Process Cognitive Memory System for Self-Evolving LLM Agents
summary: DCPM reorganizes agent memory into a cognitive capability hierarchy with dual processes for synchronous belief revision and asynchronous schema induction, improving performance on benchmarks requiring implicit cross-session inference.
rationale: The paper proposes a novel memory system architecture for LLM agents that addresses the limitations of current retrieval-based memory, focusing on belief revision and schema induction for long-term reliability.

title: MimirRAG: A Multi-Agent RAG Framework for Financial Data Retrieval with Metadata Integration
summary: MimirRAG is a multi-agent RAG system for financial data retrieval that integrates metadata, table-aware chunking, and agent-based query planning to improve accuracy and reduce hallucinations in financial analysis.
rationale: The paper describes a multi-agent RAG system with specific architectural choices (metadata integration, table-aware chunking, agentic workflow) to improve reliability and accuracy in a high-stakes domain, addressing memory and coordination aspects of trustworthy agents.

title: Mimosa Framework: Toward Evolving Multi-Agent Systems for Scientific Research
summary: Mimosa is an evolving multi-agent framework for scientific research that dynamically synthesizes and refines workflows through experimental feedback, leveraging dynamic tool discovery and providing fully logged execution traces for auditability.
rationale: The paper describes an end-to-end agent architecture with features for dynamic workflow generation, iterative refinement, and auditability, which are relevant to trustworthy agentic systems.

title: Mind the Tool Failures: Achieving Synergistic Tool Gains for Medical Agents
summary: Proposes a reinforcement learning framework for instance-level tool selection in medical agents to mitigate failures from imperfect tools and improve reliability in clinical settings.
